title: Use a Library Catalog
summary: Search a library catalog to find books and resources on a topic.
learnerPromise: You will be able to search a library catalog by topic, title, or author and find resources you can borrow or use.
whyItMatters: Library catalogs help you find reliable books and materials for school projects that may not be available online for free. Knowing how to search a catalog is a skill you can use at any library.
realLifeUses: Searching for books about a country for a geography report
Finding novels by a specific author for a book review
Locating science books at your reading level for a research project
Checking if a book is available before visiting the library
materialsNeeded: Library card or access to a library's online catalog
Device with internet access (computer, tablet, or phone)
A topic or book title to search for
result: A list of 2-3 relevant resources found in the catalog with their call numbers or locations
youWillLearn: How to do a keyword search for a topic
How to read a search result and find the call number or location
How to tell if a book is currently available or checked out
How to refine your search if you get too many or too few results
miniChallenge: Search your school or local library catalog for a topic you are studying. Find 2-3 relevant resources and write down their titles and locations.
steps: Go to your library's website and find the catalog search page.
Type a topic, title, or author name into the search box.
Look through the results and click on a promising item for details.
Write down the title, author, and call number or location.
If needed, try different keywords or use filters to narrow your results.
completionCriteria: You found at least 2 resources related to your topic
You can locate each resource using the call number or location info
You tried at least one search technique like a keyword or filter
commonProblems: Getting too many results and feeling overwhelmed
Not finding anything because the search words were too specific
Not noticing that a book is checked out until you go to the shelf
tips: Start with broad keywords and narrow down if you get too many results
Write down call numbers before going to find the book on the shelf
Ask a librarian for help if you cannot find what you need, that is what they are there for
